brackets.headline: Nailed it
brackets.body: You have overcome the odds and managed an outstanding success. You may achieve an additional bonus of your choice to the action. When dealing damage, you can choose to double it or pick another appropriate bonus effect.

brackets.headline: Success
brackets.body: You have achieved your goal without any compromises. When attacking, you hit the target and deal standard damage.

brackets.headline: Tough Choice
brackets.body: You succeed in your action, but at a cost. The Mediator gives you a Tough Choice with some kind of Setback attached. When attacking, you hit, but must make a Tough Choice.

brackets.headline: Failure
brackets.body: You have failed at what you were attempting to do. You face a Setback of the Mediator's choice. When attacking, you miss the target.

brackets.headline: Cascade Failure
brackets.body: Something has gone terribly wrong. You suffer a severe consequence of the Mediator's choice. When attacking, you miss the target and suffer a Setback chosen by the Mediator.

brackets.headline: Nailed It
brackets.body: The NPC succeeds spectacularly at their action. They get an additional bonus of the Mediator's choice. If they are making an attack, they hit, and do double damage or get another bonus of the Mediator's choice.

brackets.headline: Success
brackets.body: The NPC achieves their action successfully. An attack hits and deals standard damage.

brackets.headline: Tough Choice
brackets.body: The NPC is successful, but faces a Tough Choice. The players give the Mediator a choice between two Setbacks. In combat, a weapon attack hits, but with a choice of Setback chosen by the players.

brackets.headline: Failure
brackets.body: The NPC has failed at their action. The players choose an appropriate Setback for failure. In combat, a weapon attack misses.

brackets.headline: Cascade Failure
brackets.body: The NPC has catastrophically failed at their action. They suffer a Severe Setback of the player's choice. A weapon attack misses, with a Severe Setback chosen by the players.

brackets.headline: Reactor Overdrive
brackets.body: Your Mech's reactor goes into overdrive. Your Mech can take any additional action this turn or Push their next roll within 10 minutes for free.

brackets.headline: Reactor Overheat
brackets.body: Your Mech's reactor has overheated. Your Mech shuts down and gains the Vulnerable Trait. Your Mech will re-activate at the end of your next turn. In addition, your Mech takes an amount of SP damage equal to your current Heat.

brackets.headline: Module Overload
brackets.body: One of your Mech's Modules chosen at random or by the Mediator is destroyed.

brackets.headline: System Overload
brackets.body: One of your Mech's Systems chosen at random or by the Mediator is destroyed.

brackets.headline: Reactor Overload
brackets.body: Your Mech's reactor goes into full meltdown and explodes. Your Mech, as well as any mounted Systems, Modules, and all Cargo, is destroyed in the explosion. Everything in Close Range of your Mech takes SP damage equal to your Mech's Maximum Heat Capacity. They may take any Turn Action or Reaction in response to try to avoid this. Your Pilot dies unless they have a means to escape. The area your Mech was in becomes Irradiated.

brackets.headline: Actively Helpful and Friendly
brackets.body: The NPCs are incredibly friendly and positive towards the group and will actively help them in any reasonable way they can.

brackets.headline: Friendly
brackets.body: The NPCs are friendly and willing to talk, trade, and offer information to the group; however, they will still ask for their fair share in return.

brackets.headline: Unfriendly
brackets.body: The NPCs react in an unfriendly manner to the group; they are difficult to talk or trade with and reluctant to offer any help to the Pilots.

brackets.headline: Hostile
brackets.body: The NPCs are actively hostile to the group. They will defend their area, make motions to attack, gesture and threaten, and be unwilling to help in any way.

brackets.headline: Actively Hostile
brackets.body: The NPCs will launch an attack on the group if appropriate or flee from them, barricade themselves in, and avoid contact as though they were hostile.

brackets.headline: Jackpot!
brackets.body: You find a Mech Chassis, System, or Module at the Tech Level of the area. It is in the Damaged Condition. This can be randomised or chosen by the Mediator.

brackets.headline: Winning
brackets.body: You find 3 Scrap of the Tech Level of the area.

brackets.headline: Not Bad
brackets.body: You find 2 Scrap of the Tech Level of the area.

brackets.headline: Better than Nothing
brackets.body: You find 1 Scrap of the Tech Level of the area.

brackets.headline: Nothing
brackets.body: You find nothing in this area.

brackets.headline: Lion's Share
brackets.body: You salvage the Mech Chassis, a System and a Module of your choice mounted on it. They both have the Damaged Condition. Anything else is considered destroyed.

brackets.headline: Meat and Potatoes
brackets.body: You salvage the Mech Chassis or a System or Module of your choice mounted on it. It has the Damaged Condition. Anything else is considered destroyed.

brackets.headline: Bits and Pieces
brackets.body: You salvage a System or Module of your choice mounted on the Mech. It has the Damaged Condition. Anything else is considered destroyed.

brackets.headline: Nuts and Bolts
brackets.body: You salvage half of the Salvage Value of the Mech Chassis in Scrap of its Tech Level, to a minimum of 1. Everything else is considered destroyed.

brackets.headline: Ashes and Dust
brackets.body: The Mech is unsalvageable: its Chassis, Systems and Modules are all considered destroyed.

brackets.headline: Fight to the Death
brackets.body: The NPCs see this one through to the end. They hunker down and will not retreat from this fight under any circumstance.

brackets.headline: Keep Fighting
brackets.body: The NPCs continue to fight this one out for now.

brackets.headline: Fighting Retreat
brackets.body: The NPCs retreat, but do so whilst continuing to fight. They will fight for one more round and then retreat.

brackets.headline: Retreat
brackets.body: The NPCs flee the fight as quickly and safely as possible.

brackets.headline: Surrender
brackets.body: The NPCs surrender to whoever is attacking them. If there is nobody to surrender to, they will recklessly flee.

brackets.headline: You Shot First
brackets.body: Two Pilots chosen by the players act first. Play then passes to the NPC group and one NPC chosen by the Mediator acts next.

brackets.headline: Quickdraw
brackets.body: One Pilot chosen by the players acts first. Play then passes to the NPC group and one NPC chosen by the Mediator acts.

brackets.headline: Wait and See
brackets.body: One NPC chosen by the players acts first. Play then passes to the player group and one Pilot chosen by the players acts.

brackets.headline: Fumble
brackets.body: One NPC chosen by the Mediator acts first. Play then passes to the player group and one Pilot chosen by the players acts.

brackets.headline: Ambush
brackets.body: Two NPCs chosen by the Mediator act first. Play then passes to the player group and one Pilot is chosen by the players to act next.

brackets.headline: Perfect Escape
brackets.body: The group makes a perfect escape from the situation to any location of their choice within the Region Map and cannot be pursued.

brackets.headline: Escape
brackets.body: The group makes a safe escape from the situation to any adjacent location of their choice within the Map and cannot be pursued.

brackets.headline: Dangerous Escape
brackets.body: The group escapes to any adjacent location of their choice within the Region Map, but at a cost. They must make a Tough Choice related to the situation.

brackets.headline: Failed Escape
brackets.body: The group fails to retreat from the situation and are pinned down. They cannot retreat and must fight it out to the end.

brackets.headline: Disastrous Escape
brackets.body: The group retreat to an adjacent location of their choice within the Region Map, but at a severe cost. They suffer a Severe Setback and may be pursued.

brackets.headline: Miraculous Survival
brackets.body: Your Mech is somehow Intact. It has 1 SP and is still fully operational. Your Pilot is unharmed.

brackets.headline: Core Damage
brackets.body: Your Mech Chassis is damaged and inoperable until repaired. All mounted Systems and Modules remain Intact. Your Pilot is reduced to 0 HP unless they have some means to escape the Mech.

brackets.headline: Module Destruction
brackets.body: A Module mounted on your Mech is destroyed. This is chosen by the Mediator or at random. Your Mech Chassis is damaged and inoperable until repaired. Your Pilot is unharmed.

brackets.headline: System Destruction
brackets.body: A System mounted on your Mech is destroyed. This is chosen by the Mediator or at random. Your Mech Chassis is damaged and inoperable until repaired. Your Pilot is unharmed.

brackets.headline: Catastrophic Damage
brackets.body: The Mech, as well as any mounted Systems and Modules as well as all Cargo, is destroyed. Your Pilot dies unless they have a means to escape the Mech.

brackets.headline: Miraculous Survival
brackets.body: You survive against the odds. You have 1 HP, remain conscious and can act normally.

brackets.headline: Unconscious
brackets.body: You are stable at 0 HP, but unconscious and cannot move or take actions until you gain at least 1 HP. You will regain consciousness naturally in 1 hour and get back up with 1 HP.

brackets.headline: Minor Injury
brackets.body: You suffer a Minor Injury such as a sprain, burns, or minor concussion. Your Max HP is reduced by 1 until healed in a Tech 3-4 Med Bay. In addition, you are Unconscious. Apply the result of 11-19.

brackets.headline: Major Injury
brackets.body: You suffer a Major Injury such as permanent scarring, broken ribs, or internal injuries. Your Max HP is reduced by 2 until healed in a Tech 5-6 Med Bay. In addition, you are Unconscious. Apply the result of 11-19.

brackets.headline: Fatal Injury
brackets.body: Your Pilot suffers a fatal injury and dies.

sections.name: Tough Choice Examples
sections.body:
Default in combat — deal 1/2 Dmg or take 2 SP/HP Dmg & deal full Dmg.
Default out of combat — gain 2 Heat and succeed, or fail.

Other options:
• You hit, but damage a System or Module on your Mech.
• You hit, but an enemy gets a free attack against you.
• You hit, but activate the downside of your weapon.
• You hit, but gain additional Heat.
• You succeed in your task, but you must expend additional AP/EP.
• You hit, but must make a Heat Check.
• You betray a friend or make an enemy.
• Save a waster from dying or get to a destination faster.
• Save one wastelander from death, but not the other.
• You Salvage something powerful, but activate its downside.

sections.name: Pushing
sections.body:
• After rolling the die you may Push your Mech reactor to roll again.
• You gain 2 Heat and make a Heat Check.
• Decide before mediator describes consequences.
• Can't Push if it'd take you over your Heat Cap.
• Can only push a roll once.

Push Order:
1. Roll the Die.
2. Re-roll the Die if you decide to push. Accept the result of the second roll.
3. Resolve all of the effects of the roll.
4. Increase your Heat by 2 and roll your Heat Check.
5. If the Heat Check fails, roll on the Reactor Overload Table and resolve the outcome.

sections.name: Actions
sections.body:
Each player may do the following on their turn:
• Move and one Turn Action.

May do the following at any time:
• Any number of Free Actions or Reactions.

Action Types:
• Reaction — Instant
• Free — Instant
• Turn — 1 Min
• Short — 10 Min
• Long — 1 Hr
• Downtime — 1 Week

sections.name: Distances
sections.body:
Close Range — Within Range to launch an Attack or circle with short range weaponry.
Medium Range — Clearly see target and use standard weapons.
Long Range — See a rough outline of a target, can use long range weapons.
Far Range — Off on the horizon, can see a rough silhouette if anything. Specialised weapons can work.

sections.name: Heat
sections.body:
• Gain Heat when using Systems, Modules, and Pushing etc.
• Cannot gain Heat above your Heat Cap.
• If an action requires you to gain Heat and it would take you over your Heat Cap, you may not do that Action.
• If you start your Turn at your Heat Cap or reach your Heat Cap during the Turn, make a Heat Check.
• To make a Heat Check roll a d20. If the result is equal to or under your Mech's current Heat, your reactor has overloaded. Roll on the Reactor Overload Table.
• A roll of 20 is always a success.

sections.name: Activating and Shutting Down a Mech
sections.body:
• To power up or Shut Down a Mech is a Turn Action.
• A Mech that's Shut Down for 1 hr removes all Heat.
• A Shut Down Mech is Vulnerable (takes 2× Dmg).
• Embarking or disembarking from a Mech is a Turn Action.
• You may disembark as a Reaction if you have an escape system.

sections.name: Mech Damage
sections.body:
• Mechs take SP Dmg.
• HP Dmg = SP/2.
• When Mech SP reaches 0 roll on the Critical Damage Table.
• Mech SP is restored during Downtime in the Crawler Mech Bay.
• Systems and Modules can be Destroyed or Damaged.
• They are repaired in the Mech Bay during Downtime or using Scrap in the field.

sections.name: Pilot Damage
sections.body:
• Pilots take HP Dmg.
• SP Dmg = 2× HP.
• When Pilot HP reaches 0 roll on Critical Injury Table.
• Pilot HP is restored during Downtime in the Crawler Med Bay.
• Pilots can receive long term injuries. They are healed in the Crawler Med Bay.

sections.name: Map Movement
sections.body:
Campaign Map:
• Crawler or Pilot = 1 point in a week
• Mech = 1 point in a day

Region Map:
• Crawler or Pilot = 1 point in a day
• Mech = 1 point in an hour

Area Map:
• Crawler or Pilot = 1 point in an hour
• Mech = 1 point in 10 mins

sections.name: Downtime Procedure
sections.body:
Post-Session:
• Tally Salvage and calculate Scrap total.
• Pay Upkeep and Upgrade Crawler if enough paid.
• Restore Mech and Pilot SP and HP. Repair Systems and heal Injuries.
• Roll to find out what's available in the Trade Hall.
• Craft new Systems, Modules, and Chassis. Then customise your Mech with all the new gear.
• Train new Pilot Abilities in the Pilot Bay.
• Obtain a new piece of Pilot Equipment.

Pre-session:
• Gather Cantina rumours.
• Prepare for the salvage run.

sections.name: Safety Protocols
sections.body:
Boundaries:
• Out of Bounds — Discuss any hard limits for your game, any Player or the Mediator may specify content that is out of bounds.
• Off Camera — These topics may be included, but not detailed. Refer to these indirectly or in the abstract.

Stop Sign:
Use the Stop Sign to signal if something makes you feel uncomfortable or if you need to pause the game at any time. You do not need to justify its use.
If someone uses the Stop Sign find out why out of character and consider setting new boundaries or revisiting existing ones.

sections.name: Salvage Procedures
sections.body:
Mech Salvage:
• Use Mech Salvage Ability on a valid target.
• Roll on Mech Salvage Table.

Area Salvage:
• Use Area Salvage Ability.
• Salvage listed by Tech type and Supply e.g. T2 // Supply 3.
• Spend AP/EP to Salvage per attempt. May attempt a number of times = Supply.
• Roll on Area Salvage Table per attempt made.

sections.name: Tech Levels
sections.body:
Tech 1 — Basic industrial equipment, simple mechanisms, antique, obsolete, or scrap built weaponry.
Tech 2 — Core industrial machines, basic weaponry, standard build mechanisms and electronics.
Tech 3 — Basic military equipment, specialised industrial equipment, more refined electronics and precision machinery.
Tech 4 — Military grade weaponry and support systems, very specialised industrial gear, high tech electronics and components.
Tech 5 — Corpo mil-tech grade equipment, the very best in the field. Highly specialised industrial gear.
Tech 6 — State of the art, secret projects that begin with X, bio and nanite tech.

sections.name: Conditions
sections.body:
Intact — Working Condition, can be fitted to a Mech.
Damaged — Repair with Scrap or in Mech Bay.
Destroyed — Can't be Salvaged.

sections.name: Scrap Value
sections.body:
• 1 Scrap = 1 Cargo
• Scrap Value = No. of Scrap × Tech Level
  e.g. 2× T6 Scrap = 12× T1 Scrap
